content.subtitle: Form
items: I / you / he / she / it / we / they

content.subtitle: Example
items: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin, but **he** did it by mistake!

content.subtitle: Form
items: me / you / him / her / it / us / them

content.subtitle: Use
items: The object of a verb: Could you give **me** that equipment?
After prepositions: Could you give that equipment to **me**?

content.subtitle: Form
items: my / your / his / her / its / our / their

content.subtitle: Use
items: To show who owns or has something: That's **their** car.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: Possessive determiners are always followed by a noun.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **its** and **it's** do not mean the same thing.
Here's the dog's water and here's **its** food. (= the dog's food)
**It's** the best camera I've ever had. (= It is ...)

content.subtitle: Form
items: mine / yours / his / hers / ours / theirs

content.subtitle: Use
items: To show who owns or has something: That car is **ours**.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: Possessive pronouns are not followed by a noun.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: There is no possessive pronoun for **it**.

content.subtitle: Form
items: myself / yourself / himself / herself / itself / ourselves / yourselves / themselves

content.subtitle: Use
items: To describe actions where the subject and object are the same: My computer turns **itself** off after half an hour.
To emphasise who does something: Nobody helped me. I did it **myself**!

sections.title: A: Subject pronouns
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences with a subject pronoun.

items.sentence: Marta and I are tired. ...... need a break.
Where is Jack? ...... is in the garden.
My phone is new. ...... has a great camera.
Lucy and Anna are sisters. ...... live in Seville.
I'm late, but ...... can still catch the bus.
Tom, are ...... ready?

sections.title: B: Object pronouns
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences with an object pronoun.

items.sentence: Can you help ...... with this homework?
I called Lisa, but she didn't answer ...... .
We saw Daniel and gave ...... the tickets.
The cat is hungry. Please feed ...... .
My grandparents live nearby, so we visit ...... every weekend.
Please send ...... an email when you arrive.
Is this **my / mine** notebook?
Emma is looking for **her / hers** keys.
The dog is in **its / it's** bed.
We are doing **our / ours** project now.
Tom and Ben forgot **their / theirs** lunch.
I like **your / yours** new jacket.

sections.title: D: Possessive pronouns
sections.instructions: Complete with the correct possessive pronoun.

items.sentence: This seat is my seat. It's ...... .
That phone belongs to Sara. It's ...... .
These books are for you. They're ...... .
The blue car is for our family. It's ...... .
Those bags belong to the boys. They're ...... .
This bike belongs to my brother. It's ...... .
Be careful! You can hurt ...... with that knife.
I taught ...... to play the guitar.
They made the cake ...... .
We painted the classroom ...... .
My sister looked at ...... in the mirror.
The robot can switch ...... off.
Pedro and I are classmates. ...... sit together in English.
I can't find my keys. Have you seen ...... ?
That skateboard isn't ours. It's ...... .
Julia did the whole project by ...... .
My parents and I love ...... neighbourhood.
Where is your brother? I need to talk to ...... .

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one pronoun mistake. Write the correct word.

items.sentence: This is hers bag.
Me am very happy today.
Can you give that book to she?
The students finished the activity by ourself.
The cat is playing with it's toy.
These tickets are our.

sections.title: H: Rewrite using the word in bold
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it means the same as the first.

items.sentence:
That coat belongs to me. **MINE**
That coat is ...... .

Ana did the work without help. **HERSELF**
Ana did the work by ...... .

This is the dogs' bed. **THEIR**
This is ...... bed.

Could you send the email to Laura and me? **US**
Could you send ...... the email?

The bicycle belongs to Tom. **HIS**
The bicycle is ...... .

Nobody helped us. **OURSELVES**
We did it ...... .
